The Lessee waives any claim that the escalation under this clause exceeds the ceiling under the Telangana Buildings (Lease, Rent and Eviction) Control Act, 1960, and accepts that the enhanced rent is a reasonable and fair reflection of the market conditions at Hyderabad.
indian_law: Telangana Buildings (Lease, Rent and Eviction) Control Act, 1960; Transfer of Property Act 1882

clause_text: It is hereby recorded that with reference to this lease deed executed between Siddhi Networks Pvt. Ltd. at Lucknow and Aditya Srinivasan, engaged in the capacity of a professional in the BPO/KPO sector, the parties agree to a mid-tenancy unilateral revision of the monthly rent of Rs. 22,000/- (Rupees 22 Thousand only) by the Lessor, up to twenty per cent (20%) of the base rent, by a written notice of sixty (60) days served on the Lessee in respect of the said premises at Whitefield, Bengaluru.  Any delay by the Lessee in paying the escalated rent shall attract compound interest at twenty-four per cent (24%) per annum, which shall be compounded monthly and shall be recoverable as a debt due from the Lessee.  The Lessee waives any claim that the escalation under this clause exceeds the ceiling under the Karnataka Rent Act, 1999, and accepts that the enhanced rent is a reasonable and fair reflection of the market conditions at Bengaluru.

Notwithstanding Section 108(j) of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, the Lessee's obligation to pay the escalated rent shall be enforceable on demand, and failure to pay shall constitute a material breach giving rise to eviction without statutory notice.  The waiver of statutory protection contained herein is given as a condition precedent to the grant of tenancy, and the Lessee shall not be heard to argue otherwise in any proceeding before any forum.
indian_law: Telangana Buildings (Lease, Rent and Eviction) Control Act, 1960; Transfer of Property Act 1882

clause_text: This provision shall govern the relationship between Bhagwati Consulting LLP, a company incorporated under the laws of India and carrying on business in e-commerce at Jaipur, and Anirudh Mukherjee, and The parties have agreed that the Lessor shall not be bound by any statutory cap on rent enhancement, and shall enjoy complete discretion to revise the rent of Rs. 18,000/- (Rupees 18 Thousand only) as and when the Lessor considers appropriate.  Any statutory cap on rent enhancement under the Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001 shall be deemed inapplicable to the said premises, and the parties have specifically contracted out of such statutory protection.  The Lessee shall indemnify the Lessor against any action initiated by any statutory authority alleging breach of the Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001, and shall bear all costs incurred by the Lessor in defending such action.
indian_law: Rajasthan Rent Control Act, 2001; Transfer of Property Act 1882

clause_text: Pursuant to the engagement of Shruti Pandey by Kumbha Realty Pvt. Ltd. at its SaaS product development operations at Visakhapatnam, the Lessor reserves the absolute right to revise the monthly rent at any time during the term of this commercial lease deed, at the Lessor's sole discretion, by service of a notice of seven (7) days, and the Lessee hereby waives the protection of the Delhi Rent Act, 1995.  Notwithstanding Section 108(j) of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, the Lessee's obligation to pay the escalated rent shall be enforceable on demand, and failure to pay shall constitute a material breach giving rise to eviction without statutory notice.  The Lessee shall indemnify the Lessor against any action initiated by any statutory authority alleging breach of the Delhi Rent Act, 1995, and shall bear all costs incurred by the Lessor in defending such action.
indian_law: Delhi Rent Act, 1995; Transfer of Property Act 1882

clause_text: This clause shall apply to the engagement of Ananya Sharma with Indigo Ventures LLP at its BPO/KPO establishment at Ahmedabad, and The rent reserved under this lease deed shall be revised bi-annually by twenty per cent (20%), and the Lessee acknowledges and accepts that such revision, though contrary to the Gujarat Rent Control Act, 2011, shall bind the Lessee as a permanent condition of tenancy.  The Lessee shall not be entitled to approach the Rent Controller, the Small Causes Court or the High Court at Ahmedabad for a declaration that the escalated rent is contrary to the Gujarat Rent Control Act, 2011, and such recourse is expressly waived.  The waiver of statutory protection contained herein is given as a condition precedent to the grant of tenancy, and the Lessee shall not be heard to argue otherwise in any proceeding before any forum.
indian_law: Gujarat Rent Control Act, 2011; Transfer of Property Act 1882

clause_text: The rent reserved under this rental agreement shall be revised bi-annually by twenty per cent (20%), and the Lessee acknowledges and accepts that such revision, though contrary to the West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act, 1997, shall bind the Lessee as a permanent condition of tenancy.  The Lessee shall not be entitled to approach the Rent Controller, the Small Causes Court or the High Court at Kolkata for a declaration that the escalated rent is contrary to the West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act, 1997, and such recourse is expressly waived.  This clause shall bind the Lessee notwithstanding any amendment to the West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act, 1997 that may increase the Lessee's statutory protection in the future, and the Lessee shall not be entitled to claim the benefit of any such amendment.
indian_law: West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act, 1997; Transfer of Property Act 1882

clause_text: In respect of the leave and license agreement between Pratham India Pvt. Ltd. (having its principal place of business at Hyderabad) and Ramesh Nair engaged in the EdTech division, the Lessor reserves the absolute right to revise the monthly rent at any time during the term of this leave and license agreement, at the Lessor's sole discretion, by service of a notice of seven (7) days, and the Lessee hereby waives the protection of the Delhi Rent Act, 1995.  Notwithstanding Section 108(j) of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882, the Lessee's obligation to pay the escalated rent shall be enforceable on demand, and failure to pay shall constitute a material breach giving rise to eviction without statutory notice.  The Lessor shall be entitled to enforce the escalated rent by self-help, including re-entry on the said premises without judicial process, if the Lessee fails to pay the enhanced amount.
indian_law: Delhi Rent Act, 1995; Transfer of Property Act 1882

clause_text: In the context of the services rendered by Manish Kapoor to Matrix Labs Pvt. Ltd., a company engaged in real estate with its registered office at Jaipur, the parties have agreed that The rent reserved under this rental agreement shall be revised bi-annually by twenty per cent (20%), and the Lessee acknowledges and accepts that such revision, though contrary to the West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act, 1997, shall bind the Lessee as a permanent condition of tenancy.  Any statutory cap on rent enhancement under the West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act, 1997 shall be deemed inapplicable to the said premises, and the parties have specifically contracted out of such statutory protection.  The Lessor shall be entitled to enforce the escalated rent by self-help, including re-entry on the said premises without judicial process, if the Lessee fails to pay the enhanced amount.
indian_law: West Bengal Premises Tenancy Act, 1997; Transfer of Property Act 1882
